mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth signals strengthening aggregate demand, which improves corporate revenue expectations and drives positive equity returns over the following quarter as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in > 50% of instances where GDPC1 growth exceeds the threshold, or predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in the unemployment rate and declines in nonfarm payrolls indicate labor market deterioration, which reduces consumer spending power and corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample instances, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and robust supply-side activity, which supports corporate margins and drives positive equity returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no significant difference between high and low INDPRO growth regimes (p > 0.05), or the signal's directional accuracy falls below 52% over a 2-year test period.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, tight monetary conditions compress equity risk premiums and increase borrowing costs for leveraged firms, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal exhibits no predictive power after controlling for the VIX term structure.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and increased consumer credit balances indicate robust household balance sheets, which sustain consumption demand and generate positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between RSAFS growth and 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.10 for three consecutive years, or forward returns in high-savings periods do not outperform low-savings periods by at least 1.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts fall while unemployment rises signals weakening demand for new construction and broader economic slowdown, which reduces corporate investment and drives negative equity returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp deceleration in industrial production relative to historical trends indicates supply chain bottlenecks or capacity constraints, which compresses profit margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly lower during INDPRO deceleration episodes compared to baseline, or the signal's hit rate drops below 48% over a 12-month walk-forward test.
